entry.title: Re: SAP OData to MCP Server running on BTP
entry.content:
Hi, if have an issue to connect claude.ai to the mcp. I deploy the app,  test pattern also working.

POST http://<myurl>/config/services/test
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "serviceNames": ["ZBP_EMPLOYEE_CDS", "API_BUSINESS_PARTNER", "TEMP_TEST_SRV"]
}

I also assigned the roles/role collection (MCPAdmin, MCPViewer, MCPEditor) to my user. When I try to connect the MCP with claude.ai, the SAP Login mask appears

When I add the credentials,  I get the following error.

Any idea what is missing for the authorization or were to get more details about the error?

Thanks in advance.

Miri

entry.title: Personas 3.0 : How to add functionality in table cell
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

 What we'd like to achieve is as follows;
 1. Create a new column in the Overview table of transaction PA20.
 2. Depends on value, change color of cell in that new column. 
 3. When above cell is clicked, jump to another screen.

Can we achive it if we use SAPUI5 applet easily?

Thank you
Yuko

entry.title: Managing SAP AI Core from the Terminal with saic-cli
entry.content:
Managing SAP AI Core from the Terminal with saic-cli

Working with SAP AI Core day-to-day surfaces a few recurring pain points with SAP AI Launchpad:

  • No way to create custom scenarios through the UI
  • No bulk operations for prompt templates — creating or cleaning up templates means doing it one by one
  • Repetitive UI navigation for routine tasks like listing configurations or checking deployment status
  • No scripting support — any automation requires building custom API calls from scratch

saic-cli is a small CLI tool that brings all of this to the terminal — no UI, no manual API calls, just commands.

Getting Started

1. Install

npm install -g sap-aicore-cli

2. Connect to Your AI Core Instance

The setup command wraps cf login and stores your credentials:

saic-cli setup --sso -a https://api.cf.<region>.hana.ondemand.com

This is the only configuration you need. From here, every command picks up your credentials automatically.

What Makes It Useful

Beyond the commands themselves, a few things make saic-cli practical for day-to-day work:

Full API coverage — deployments, executions, configurations, prompt templates, secrets, repositories, resource groups, dataset files, and more. If it's in the SAP AI Core REST API, there's a command for it.

Zero config after setup — one saic-cli setup command is all it takes. Every subsequent command picks up credentials automatically, no extra configuration needed.

Scriptable by default — every command supports --json output, making it easy to pipe results into other tools or use in shell scripts.

Safe to automate — --dry-run lets you preview any write operation before it runs, useful in CI/CD pipelines.

Manage Deployments

# List running deployments
saic-cli list-deployments --status RUNNING

# Create a deployment from a configuration
saic-cli create-deployment --config-id <config-id>

# Stop and clean up
saic-cli update-deployment <id> --target-status STOPPED
saic-cli delete-deployment <id> --force

Configure Foundation Models

Creating a configuration for a foundation model like GPT-4o is a single command:

saic-cli create-configuration \
  --name "gpt-4o" \
  --executable-id "azure-openai" \
  --scenario-id "foundation-models" \
  --params '[{"key":"modelName","value":"gpt-4o"}]'

Manage Prompt Templates in Bulk

One area where the Launchpad falls short is prompt template management. saic-cli provides a small workflow to go from zero to a deployed template quickly.

Start by generating a ready-to-edit template scaffold:

saic-cli generate-template

This creates a folder with everything you need:

my-template/
├── prompt.yaml         # Main config: name, version, scenario, variables, LLM settings
├── system-prompt.md    # System role instructions (edit to define AI behavior)
└── user-prompt.md      # User role template with variable placeholders e.g. {{?InputText}}

The prompt.yaml looks like this:

name: MY_TEMPLATE
version: 1.0.0
scenario: MY_SCENARIO

spec:
  template:
    - role: system
      contentFile: ./system-prompt.md
    - role: user
      contentFile: ./user-prompt.md
  variable_definition:
    InputText: ""
    OutputFormat: ""
  additional_fields:
    executableId: azure-openai
    llmName: gpt-4o

Edit the files to fit your use case, then do a dry run before deploying:

saic-cli create-prompt --dir ./my-template --dry-run

Once ready, deploy:

saic-cli create-prompt --dir ./my-template

You can also list and clean up templates by scenario:

# List templates in a scenario
saic-cli list-templates --scenario-id my-scenario

# Delete all templates in a scenario
saic-cli delete --scenario my-scenario

Scripting & Automation

Every command supports --json output, which makes it easy to pipe results into other tools:

# Get all running deployments as JSON
saic-cli list-deployments --status RUNNING --json

# Preview a command before running it
saic-cli create-deployment --config-id <id> --dry-run

The --dry-run flag is particularly useful in CI/CD pipelines — you can validate inputs without making any changes.

Other Commands

Beyond deployments and prompts, the CLI covers:

  • Executions & schedules — create, update, delete, and schedule AI executions
  • Secrets — manage Docker registry, object store, and generic secrets
  • Repositories & applications — onboard git repos and manage ArgoCD applications
  • Resource groups & services — full lifecycle management
  • Dataset files — upload, download, and delete files from object storage

Run saic-cli --help to see the full list.

Project Structure

saic-cli is a TypeScript CLI where each command is implemented as a self-contained plugin. Commands are lazy-loaded, so only what's needed gets initialized. Under the hood it uses the @sap-ai-sdk packages to talk to SAP AI Core.

sap-aicore-cli/
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts                  # Entry point, command routing
│   ├── index.ts                # Library exports
│   ├── commands/               # Command plugins (one file per command)
│   │   ├── setup.ts            # Interactive setup wizard
│   │   ├── deployments.ts      # Deployment CRUD
│   │   ├── executions.ts       # Execution management
│   │   ├── create-prompt.ts    # Prompt template creation
│   │   ├── generate-template.ts# Template scaffold generator
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── api/                    # API wrappers (one file per resource)
│   │   ├── deployments.ts
│   │   ├── prompt-registry.ts
│   │   └── ...
│   └── utils/                  # Shared utilities

Closing Thoughts

saic-cli is still early-stage and covers common day-to-day use cases — but if you're working with SAP AI Core and find yourself reaching for the same Launchpad screens repeatedly, it might save you some clicks.

Feedback and contributions are welcome.

Links

  • npm: sap-aicore-cli
  • GitHub: mduw/sap-aicore-cli
  • SAP AI Core REST APIs

entry.title: Re: Destination HU not derived
entry.content: Hi @Vikas Hi, Were you able to resolve this issue? If yes, could you please let me know what exactly was done to fix it? I am also facing the same problem where, for multiple materials packed into a single HU, the destination HU is not getting determined during putaway. Any guidance would be really helpful. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Building an AI-Powered Chemical Quality Management System: Part 4 - Deploying to BTP Cloud Found
entry.content:
@ranjeetkumar07 - Thanks for sharing your experience and writing the blog ...
Just feedback - In your blog content.. could you adjust your code-block into code snippnet that looks good for others to read it well and more blog readers can copy and understand it better.

entry.title: Re: Travel Under Quarantine(TUQ): A supply chain concept of utilizing the quarantine time in Shippin
entry.content: Yes, this concept is widely used in many settings. For example, in the medtech industry, it is applied when devices are sent for sterilization—there is no need to return them to the original site; the products can be shipped directly to the distribution center.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 + ESM - API Authentication Methods
entry.content:
@JainSanket 

For Inbound -  Communication System which is already configured only works for Inbound Data Connector APIs only. So same credentials you cannot use it for cases, leads, apppointments .... etc for those APIs.. 

Using Technical User and Technical Role having right permissions is the right way but for Production.. user OAuth2Password, OAuth2SAMLBearer or OAuth2JWT.  No Basic Authentication is recommended.

entry.title: Extracting Structured Knowledge from Document (Sales SOPs) Using LLM— A Plug-and-Play Python App
entry.content:
What You Will Build : 

 

 

A single-page web application where you can:

  - Upload a PDF or DOCX Sales SOP document

  - Click Extract Entities — Claude reads the document and returns structured JSON

  - Browse entities grouped into 4 categories: People & Roles, Products & Services, Processes & Steps, Systems & Tools

  - Add missing entities manually via a Fiori dialog

  - Delete incorrect entries

  - Works on both text-layer PDFs and scanned/image PDFs (OCR fallback)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Architecture Overview

The stack is intentionally minimal:

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prerequisites

  System dependencies (install once):

  # macOS

  brew install tesseract poppler

  # Ubuntu / Debian

  sudo apt install tesseract-ocr poppler-utils

  - Python 3.10 or higher

  - An Anthropic API key or SAP AI Core service credentials (see section below)

  Project Structure

  sales-sop-extractor/

  ├── app.py                    # Flask app, all routes

  ├── services/

  │   ├── document_parser.py    # PDF + DOCX → text (with OCR fallback)

  │   ├── entity_extractor.py   # Claude API integration

  │   └── storage.py            # In-memory session store

  ├── static/

  │   └── app.js                # Frontend state and API calls

  ├── templates/

  │   └── index.html            # SAP Fiori single-page UI

  ├── requirements.txt

  └── .env.example

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Step 1 — Clone and Install

  git clone https://github.tools.sap/<MY SAP ID >/document_extractor

  cd sales-sop-extractor

  python3 -m venv venv

  source venv/bin/activate

  pip install -r requirements.txt

 

 requirements.txt:

  flask==3.0.3

  python-dotenv==1.0.1

  anthropic>=0.49.0

  pdfplumber==0.11.4

  python-docx==1.1.2

  pdf2image==1.17.0

  pytesseract==0.3.13

  gunicorn==22.0.0

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Step 2 — Configure Your API Connection

Copy the example env file:

  cp .env.example .env

  Option A — Anthropic API (direct)

  <html>
<code>
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-your-key-here<br>
FLASK_SECRET_KEY=change-this-in-production<br>
FLASK_DEBUG=true<br>
MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB=20
</code>
</html>

  Option B — SAP AI Core on BTP

If you are running this inside an SAP BTP environment, you can connect through SAP AI Core instead of calling the Anthropic API directly. Replace the entity extractor's HTTP call with the AI Core OAuth2 + inference endpoint:

  AICORE_AUTH_URL=https://<subaccount>.authentication.sap.hana.ondemand.com

  AICORE_CLIENT_ID=<client-id>

  AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET=<client-secret>

  AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP=default

  AICORE_BASE_URL=https://api.ai.<region>.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com/v2

  AICORE_MODEL=claude-opus-4-6

The entity_extractor.py service handles the OAuth2 client-credentials flow and token caching automatically when these variables are present:

  def _get_access_token() -> str:

      auth_url = os.environ["AICORE_AUTH_URL"].rstrip("/") + "/oauth/token"

      resp = requests.post(

          auth_url,

          data={"grant_type": "client_credentials"},

          auth=(os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_ID"], os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET"]),

          timeout=15,

      )

      resp.raise_for_status()

      data = resp.json()

      return data["access_token"]

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Step 3 — Run the App

  python3 app.py

  Open http://localhost:5000 in your browser.

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  How It Works

  Document Parsing — Text Layer + OCR Fallback

  The parser first attempts to extract the text layer using pdfplumber. If fewer than 50 characters are returned (indicating a scanned or image-only PDF), it automatically falls back to OCR:

  def parse_pdf(file_bytes: bytes) -> tuple[str, bool]:

      # 1. Try text layer first

      text_parts = []

      with pdfplumber.open(io.BytesIO(file_bytes)) as pdf:

          for page in pdf.pages:

              page_text = page.extract_text()

              if page_text:

                  text_parts.append(page_text)

      text = "\n".join(text_parts)

 

      # 2. Fall back to OCR if text layer is empty

      if len(text.strip()) < 50:

          images = convert_from_bytes(file_bytes, dpi=300)

          text = "\n".join(

              pytesseract.image_to_string(img.convert("L"))

              for img in images

          )

          return text, True  # ocr_used=True

 

      return text, False

  When OCR is used, an orange 📷OCR badge appears in the sidebar so the user knows the document was image-based.

  Entity Extraction — Claude Prompt Design

  The system prompt is kept tight and unambiguous:

  SYSTEM_PROMPT = (

      "You are an expert business analyst specializing in sales processes. "

      "Extract named entities from Sales SOP documents. "

      "Respond with valid JSON only — no explanation, no markdown, no code fences."

  )

  The user prompt asks Claude to return a JSON array with category, name, and description fields across four categories:

  - people_roles — job titles, teams, functions

  - products_services — products, SKUs, pricing tiers

  - processes_steps — sales stages, workflow steps, approvals

  - systems_tools — CRM, platforms, software mentioned

Documents are truncated to 80,000 characters before sending to stay within context limits. The response is parsed defensively — code fences are stripped, each item is validated, and a UUID is assigned:

  def _parse_response(raw: str) -> list[dict]:

      text = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", raw.strip())

      text = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", text).strip()

      data = json.loads(text)

      return [

          {

              "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),

              "category": item["category"],

              "name": item["name"],

              "description": item.get("description"),

              "source": "extracted",

          }

          for item in data

          if item.get("name") and item.get("category") in VALID_CATEGORIES

      ]

 UI

  <link rel="stylesheet"

  href="https://unpkg.com/@sap/fundamental-styles@0.30.0/dist/fundamental-styles.css" />

  <link rel="stylesheet"

  href="https://unpkg.com/@sap-theming/theming-base-content/content/Base/baseLib/sap_horizon/css_variables.css" />

The layout uses fd-shell, fd-table, fd-busy-indicator, fd-dialog, and fd-object-status — giving it a genuine Fiori Horizon look without any SAP system dependency.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Testing with a Scanned PDF

To test the OCR path without needing a real scanned document, the repo includes a script that generates one. It renders text onto a PDF with reportlab, rasterizes the pages to images with pdf2image, adds a slight scanner-like blur, then re-embeds the images as a PDF with no text layer:

venv/bin/python3 generate_scanned_pdf.py

  # Creates: sample_scanned_sop.pdf (2 pages, ~250 KB, zero text layer)

Upload this file and you will see:

  - The orange 📷OCR badge in the sidebar

  - All entities extracted correctly despite no text layer

  Test results from the sample document:

28 entities extracted. Zero missed.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

API Reference

The backend exposes a clean REST API, so it can be consumed by other tools or integrated into existing SAP workflows:

Example — upload and extract via curl:

  # Upload

  UPLOAD_ID=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5000/upload \

    -F "file=@my_sop.pdf" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['upload_id'])")

  # Extract

  curl -s -X POST http://localhost:5000/extract \

    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

    -d "{\"upload_id\": \"$UPLOAD_ID\"}"

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Known Limitations:

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Running in Production

For SAP BTP Cloud Foundry deployment, add a manifest.yml and push as a standard Python buildpack app. Point AICORE_* environment variables to your AI Core service binding.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------Summary

In this blog post we built a complete, plug-and-play Sales SOP entity extractor:

  - Flask + Claude for AI-powered structured extraction

  - pdfplumber + pytesseract for universal PDF support (text layer and scanned)

  - SAP Fiori Fundamentals via CDN for a professional UI with zero SAP infrastructure

  - SAP AI Core ready — swap one environment variable block to route through BTP

The full source code is structured to be dropped into any Python environment and running in under 5 minutes. It is equally at home as a standalone internal tool or as a microservice feeding structured SOP data into larger SAP workflows.

I hope this is useful for anyone working with Sales knowledge management/ application development on the SAP ecosystem. Feel free to extend it — export to SAP Knowledge Base, feed entities into SAP CRM, or pipe the output into SAP Analytics Cloud.

Questions and feedback welcome in the comments below.

 Happy building!

entry.title: Re: Extracting Structured Knowledge from Document (Sales SOPs) Using LLM— A Plug-and-Play Python App

Great work @Thakur_Avinash 

Really like how you’ve combined AI extraction with SAP-ready integration in such a simple, plug-and-play setup.

Very practical and useful. Keep sharing more such insights!

entry.title: External Sales Commission - Single Sales Deal
entry.content:
We try to set up the Process Variant of a Single Sales Deal - External Commission. Unfortunately it never gets picked up in the Contract Settlement.

We have added Pricing Condition RCD2 in the Sales Order Pricing Schema. Do we need to add an Accrual Account Key here? We have tried blank, OC1 and OC4 but non was successful.

In the Condition contract we have added RCA1 as condition Type Fixed Amount. The Business Volume Selection Criteria have been set.

We have tried partial and final settlement run. It never did pick up the value from the sales order condition entered. Condition were copied to the billing document and the billig document has been paid.

Any hints, where we might miss a step in the set up?

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Columbus Ohio – Spring 2026
entry.content:
We are bringing SAP Inside Track to Ohio!

Whether you are a seasoned functional expert, a curious developer, an architect, or an SAP partner, this is your chance to connect, share, and learn in a relaxed, collaborative environment right here in Columbus.

Following the global momentum of SAP innovation, we are excited to gather our local community to discuss the latest in BTP, Clean Core, and the evolution of Business AI. This event is built by the community, for the community.

📅 Event Details

  • Date: Mid-May 2026 (Final date to be confirmed soon – Mark your calendars!)

  • Time: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM EST

  • Location: 200 E Campus View Blvd, STE 200, Columbus, OH 43235

  • Format: In-person

💡 What to Expect

We are currently curating an agenda focused on real-world SAP applications and future-ready technologies. Our core themes for this track include:

  • Business AI & Joule: Moving beyond the hype into practical use cases for AI in SAP.
  • Use of SAP in US-based Industries: Some realistic case studies of SAP S4HANA Implementation in USA
  • Sessions from Industry Experts

🎤 Call for Speakers

Are you working on an interesting project? Have you solved a complex integration puzzle or mastered a new SAP module? We want to hear from you!

We are looking for local speakers to share 20–40 minute sessions. Interested in presenting? Please comment below or reach out to the organizers directly!

🚗 Logistics

  • Parking: On-site parking is available at the Campus View Blvd location.

  • Travelers: For those driving in from Cleveland, Cincinnati, or out of state, there are several hotels nearby in the Crosswoods/Worthington area.

Don’t miss out on the first premier grass-roots SAP event in Central Ohio for 2026. Let's make the Columbus SAP community stronger than ever!

Questions? Post them in the comments below.

entry.title: Error When Reverse GI. Stock Stuck in Blocked Stock In Transit
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have an issue.
When I PGI for delivery, only movement type 687 was created. I didn't see the 601.

I checked stock material, it moved into Blocked Stock with Special stock T.


That's why when I want to reverse my GI, it shows error


How can I solve this?
Thank you.

#Sales
#SAPS/4HANACLoudPublicEdition

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM vs. SAP Solution Manager: Key Terminology Differences
entry.content: Good content

entry.title: Requested Delivery Date copied from Quotation to Sales Order
entry.content:
Hallo,

We are currently working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation project and have encountered an issue regarding the behavior of the Requested Delivery Date during document flow from Quotation to Quantity Contract to Sales Order.
When a Sales Order is created with reference to a Quantity Contract, the Requested Delivery Date from the Quotation is automatically copied to the Contract and then to Sales Order header.
Issue:
The business requirement is that the Requested Delivery Date on Sales Order header should be blank by default, so that users can manually select the appropriate date in the Sales Order.
In our process, Sales Orders are often created several weeks after the Quotation has been accepted, and therefore the date inherited from the Quotation is typically no longer relevant.
However:

  • The system always inherits the Requested Delivery Date from the predecessor without the possibility to suppress it.
  • We are not able to identify any relevant BAdI or configuration option that allows us to influence or clear this field on header level during document creation. 
  • For item level, we have identified a potentia

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Package in very first part of LJ Acq...
entry.content: That worked! Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Summing 2 custom fields values (Performance Form -PMGM )
entry.content: HI ,Sure... I am working on a Performance Management form (PMGM). I have created two custom numeric fields (for example: "Score 1" and "Score 2") at a custom section. I would like to automatically calculate a third custom field (for example: "Total Score") by summing these two fields. My goal is to have the "Total Score" field auto-populated dynamically based on the values entered in the two other fields, without manual input. I would like to know: If this is possible using business rules or any standard configuration Or if it requires another approach.

entry.title: Re: Summing 2 custom fields values (Performance Form -PMGM )
entry.content: HI ,Sure... I have created two custom numeric fields (for example: "Score 1" and "Score 2") at a custom section. I would like to automatically calculate a third custom field (for example: "Total Score") by summing these two fields. My goal is to have the "Total Score" field auto-populated dynamically based on the values entered in the two other fields, without manual input. I would like to know: If this is possible using business rules or any standard configuration Or if it requires another approach.

entry.title: Validation of document item text does not work with app "Create Supplier Invoice"
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts!

I have created a validation rule to ensure that a line item text is entered for every document line item:

Validation works perfectly in ‘Create Incoming Invoice’ and ‘Post General Journal Entries’. However, in ‘Create Supplier Invoice’, the configured error occurs during simulation even when all line item texts have been entered:

Vendor item:

G/L item:

 

Simulation

Is this maybe a program error? Or is the validation setup incorrect?

Thank you in advance for any hint!

Best regards

Rado

entry.title: How to handle two different pay components in a single salary review form?
entry.content: Our client uses two salary pay components: one for annual salaried employees and another for hourly salaried employees. For the annual salary review, I created the Current Salary (curSalary) mapping, and it is working as expected for annual salaried employees. how to handle hourly pay components in a same form?

entry.title: Re: Does HANA Planviz from HANA Studio execute a temporar...
entry.content: Hi Craig, Thank you for your reply. The procedure must be PLANVIZ_ACTION but the audit log doesn't actually show the procedure name. It just says EXECUTE for EVENT_ACTION. I checked the document to which you linked but it doesn't mention the procedure PLANVIZ_ACTION. I've been searching for an FAQ or document which says that PLANVIZ_ACTION gets call whenever yoy execute a PLANVIZ from HANA Studio.

entry.title: AI in the Toolbox: Exploring VS Code for Fiori Prompt Generation
entry.content:
Microsoft has rolled out some impressive new coding‑assistance capabilities across its AI offerings. Since VS Code and GitHub are already common tools in Fiori development, it’s great to see them gaining even more intelligence baked in.

In my day‑to‑day work, I still jump between BAS, the old WebIDE (yes—some landscapes still use it!), and VS Code, depending on the client setup and what the project calls for. So it made sense to explore how these new AI features fit into that mix.

This is largely an exploratory exercise—not an attempt to replace Joule. Joule remains SAP’s primary AI platform and will continue to be the preferred option where available. But there are environments where Joule isn’t accessible, and in those cases it’s worth understanding what Microsoft’s tooling can offer.

The following is one use-case for prompt generation that I've been playing with.

Store prompts in .github/prompts/ in your repository. You can type /fiori-smartapp in VSCode so Copilot generates your scaffold.

 

# Name: fiori-smartapp
# Description: Generate a UI5 1.76 CRUD page with SmartFilterBar+SmartTable and controller.

**Task**
Create:
1) `webapp/view/Orders.view.xml` – SmartFilterBar bound to `OrdersSet`, SmartTable (growing=false), toolbar with Refresh, Export.
2) `webapp/controller/Orders.controller.js` – load model, table binding, `onRefresh`, `onExport`, MessageManager error handling, `callFunction` wrapper that correlates batch errors to line items.
3) Unit test skeleton in `webapp/test/unit/Orders.controller.js`.

**Constraints**
- Framework: SAPUI5 1.76 (compatibility mode), OData V2.
- Use stable IDs; add i18n keys for all labels.
- Follow MVC; no busy‑waiting; use Promises.

**Output format**
Provide the 3 files with full code blocks.

The capability stacks well with other organization‑wide tools and use cases, making it extremely useful. It can remove a significant amount of grunt work from the development loop—though it’s clear that humans must remain firmly in the process. I’m genuinely amazed that with only modest investment in strategy and implementation, AI tools can deliver a generational leap in code generation, architecture, QA, refactoring, and documentation.

Happy coding!

entry.title: Re: How to mass printing in app "Account Document Output - China" ?
entry.content: I am sorry. Where can i find the Output Management group.Thanks!

entry.title: Re: 【保存版】Joule for Consultants (J4C) セルフサービス導入・完全ステップガイド
entry.content:
@MikuKawanabe 
ガイドありがとうございます！
必要なロールが複数ある中で、Joule_Consultant_Userを見落としてつけ忘れてうまく動かなかったりしたので、このガイドを先に見ていれば…と思いました。

ミッションがあるのも知りませんでした。↑のリンク、私はつながらなくて、検索したら以下のURLでした
https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4633/4922/

あと、購入した後、SAP for Me から有効化を依頼しないと Entitlement が選択できませんでした。StoreでAI Unit購入してから、有効化できる状態になるまでにも1週間ちょっとかかったので、購入したらすぐ使える！ではないのは注意しなきゃなと思いました（←待ちきれずに先に有効化依頼のケースを起票してしまい、あなたはライセンスもっていないよ、と言われた人）

entry.title: Use which business contexts for the Custom Fields in inbound delivery
entry.content:
Dear SAP,
I need to add custom fields to the Inbound Delivery but missing the way of adding custom field in the apps of inbound delivery.
Also, in app Custom Fields(Fiori ID F1481), there is no inbound-delivery relevant business context

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Use which business contexts for the Custom Fields in...
entry.content:
To help you add Custom Fields for delivery documents, you can kindly use the following business contexts:

  • Shipping: Delivery
  • Shipping: Delivery Item

(Please Note, the above business contexts apply to all delivery documents (including outbound delivery and inbound delivery). SAP currently does not support business contexts specifically for inbound delivery only.)

You can kindly refer to the SAP standard documentation: App Extensibility for Delivery Documents | SAP Help Portal to know more details about Custom Fields for delivery documents.

entry.title: authorizations Issue
entry.content:
Our customer has S user (S-user ID: SPHONE_NUMBER) used to submit a request for the SOC report every year for external audit purposes.

This time, she is unable to complete the same process because she receives a message indicating that she does not have the required authorizations.

When she clicks on “Request Authorization,” the request is sent back to herself, and she is unable to approve it.

I have attached a screenshot of the section for which she requires access.

Could you please assist and grant her the necessary authorizations?

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: authorizations Issue
entry.content: Please ask your super admin to help assign authorization " Access Compliance Documents " as per 2891528 - How to use the Self-Service Authorization Request - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Enhancements to Event-Based Variance Calculation with OP2025
entry.content:
The reintroduction of mixed price variances and scrap variances brings event-based processing meaningfully closer to functional parity with the classic approach, and the extension of ACDOCP to capture procurement alternative-level detail is especially important. That part of your post stood out to me.

I'd like to share an observation from a live implementation in very large-scale discrete electronics manufacturing.

In this environment, the main challenge appears one layer upstream of variance categorization itself. We see orders whose actual costs depend on outputs from other orders that are also still being settled within the same period, creating cross-order cost dependencies that cannot be cleanly resolved in sequence. Shared intermediates are consumed across multiple concurrent orders, and the actual cost state of a given cost object is still evolving at the point when variance logic is already being applied to orders that depend on it.

In that setting, the variance split can be internally consistent within the current model, yet still be assigned to cost objects whose own actual cost is not yet settled. The calculation may be formally correct within SAP's framework, but those inter-order dependencies push beyond the boundary conditions under which object-level results remain economically stable.

We have not seen this resolved through configuration or settlement design choices alone, so it seems closer to an architectural boundary condition than to a project-specific setup issue.

Has this class of inter-order dependency come up in your customer conversations as a scenario where the current cost object model reaches its limits — and if so, how has SAP typically framed it?

entry.title: Re: How to upload scope item per LoB to SAP Cloud ALM with DDA
entry.content: I have two countries in scope for my project. If I select Global in the country selection, I am getting too many errors. Do we have to upload the DDA file for each country?

entry.title: Cannot save regular items as favourites in sap for me
entry.content:
Within or SAP for Me portal we have a few resources which we use on a daily basis and navigating from 1 to other takes a lot of time in form of clicks

We cannot find a way to save these resources as a favourite list anywhere in the portal so that we can open them with 1 click

We tried to follow note DIGITS but it does not help cos the items we want to assign as favourites does not have the * button to do so example (a) Reporting-->Private cloud workspace (b) Finance & Legal --> Enterprise Agreements (c) Services and Support-->Service Requests & Cases (d) Systems and Provisioing --> Private cloud --> System Calendar (e) Services & Support --> Remote Connections 

These are a few items which we would like on our home page so that we can open them from home page instead of browsing through different options

Is this a defect in portal design, or a is it by choice or we are missing something

thanks and regards

Saket Diwan


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to enable auto -reversing function for the manual...
entry.content: Hi Chris, Thanks for the proposed solution, we tested it, the auto reverse can be implemented in current period. We do have a follow up question: in case we setup a reversal date which is not in the open period (Let's say, I setup in April 2026 now, but the period of April 2026 is not open), will the job failed and terminated and the following reverse in this job will not be implemented(even their periods are open)?

entry.title: Re: ME59N processing Purchase Requisitions that do not ex...
entry.content:
ME59N reads PRs from EBAN at job start. Yes, PRs can be selected but deleted before processing and no "temporary PRs” exist. Missing PRs usually point to deletions or custom logic. Enhancements (user exits/BAdIs) or background jobs can cause inconsistencies.

Perform the below actions to find out the root cause

entry.title: Re: ME59N processing Purchase Requisitions that do not exist in EBAN table
entry.content: You have used Public Cloud tag but your post says On-Premise. Which one is yours. Solution might differ based on this

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content: Week 3 Completed - Package ZAC_2286119.

entry.title: Re: Implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in an SAP Environment
entry.content:
Great article on implementing RAG in an SAP environment.

One key aspect is ensuring strong data governance and proper data lifecycle management to maintain accuracy and compliance. Without well-structured and clean data, even advanced RAG systems may not deliver reliable results.

Additionally, integrating enterprise data platforms can help streamline data access, improve scalability, and support better performance.

Curious to know how others are addressing data quality and scalability challenges in similar implementations.

entry.title: Re: Event alert issue in CloudALM-Job monitoring
entry.content: Hi Ellie,I could not attach the document,as only this job configured for "Critical Application Status"we could not switch off as it is production system

entry.title: Re: Error When Reverse GI. Stock Stuck in Blocked Stock I...
entry.content:
This is not a bug but a configuration issue. Movement type 687 posts stock into blocked transit, often used in returns, quality issues, or special scenarios. If this was not intended, it suggests either:

  • Wrong delivery type used (e.g., returns delivery instead of standard outbound).
  • Wrong item category determination.

Check what item category is there in delivery. Then go to 0184 and see against which delivery type, this item category is used. Next go to vov7, select that item category and see whether any special stock indicator is set. If item category is correct but the movement type is wrong, then, check the corresponding sales order which schedule line category is flowing. Go to vov5, check whether the right combination of item category to schedule line is configured. These are the root causes for your issue.

entry.title: Re: Two different company codes have two different plants...

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content: Week2 Challenge Completed

entry.title: 1H 2026 Release Alert: New Granular Permissions for TIH & GP Import/Export using Integration Center
entry.content:
As part of our ongoing commitment to data security and privacy, the 1H 2026 Release introduces a more granular Role-Based Permission (RBP) framework for the Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) and Growth Portfolio (GP).

If your organization uses the Integration Center to automate the import or export of talent data, this update requires your immediate attention to prevent scheduled job failures.

What is Changing?

Previously, integration permissions for TIH were broader. Starting with the 1H 2026 release, we are introducing specific permissions that connects administrative actions with object-level access. This ensures that only authorized administrators can move sensitive talent data in and out of the system.

Action Required: Update Your RBP Settings

To ensure your integrations continue to run without interruption, please audit and update your administrator roles in your Preview environment according to the requirements below.

1. The Mandatory Permissions

Regardless of whether you are importing or exporting, the following administrative settings are now required:

  • [NEW] Administrator Permissions —> Manage Talent Intelligence Hub → Import/Export Talent Intelligence Hub and Growth Portfolio Data

  • Administrator Permissions —> Manage Talent Intelligence Hub → View Manage Talent Intelligence Hub Settings

  • Administrator Permissions —> Manage Integration Tools → Integration Center

2. Object-Level Access (The Fine Print)

In addition to the administrative toggles above, the system now checks for specific object permissions to ensure the user has the right to see or modify the underlying data.

TaskRequired User Permission (Object-Level)Applicable Objects
Exporting DataView PermissionGrowth Portfolio, Attributes, and other TIH Objects
Importing DataCreate PermissionGrowth Portfolio, Attributes, and other TIH Objects

Recommended Next Steps

  1. Review Integration Center Logs: Identify all jobs currently targeting TIH or Growth Portfolio entities.

  2. Audit Service Users: Check the RBP roles assigned to the "Execution User" of these integrations.

  3. Test in Preview: Apply the new Import/Export Talent Intelligence Hub and Growth Portfolio Data permission in your Preview instance immediately following the 1H 2026 release.

  4. Verify: Run a "Test" execution in Integration Center to confirm that the "Permission Denied" errors are resolved.

  5. Prepare for May: During the 1H 2026 Production Release, ensure your instance is updated on Day 1 to avoid any service interruptions for your scheduled jobs.

Keeping your data secure is a collaborative effort, and these more granular controls provide the precision needed for modern data governance.

Have questions about these RBP changes? Leave a comment below or join our upcoming Release Q&A webinar!

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content: Week3 Challenge Completed

entry.title: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Congratulations to everyone who came along with us till the 4th week of the ABAP Developer Challenge. So far we have seen :

  • How to create a OData UI Service from Scratch using Joule Chat
  • How to use Joule - Explain feature to understand a block of code.
  • How to use Joule - RAP Predict Business Logic to create logic for specific tasks.

Let us wrap up with another interesting feature of Joule's ABAP AI Capability - The Joule Predictive Code Completion.

Quick Links

Please use this separate thread to ask your questions and discuss issues. 

Sample Scenario

We had asked you to create a field of type 'Date' during the RAP application creation.

Now let us use Determination function to calculate the number of years since the value in Date field. You can implement it while creating a new record using the app.

Task1: Create a determination and implement the corresponding method.

  1. Define a determination in the BDEF of the CDS view.Define Determination
  2. Now you can notice a small icon to the left of the determination definition. Click on it to create the implementation method for adding determination logic in the local handler class of the behavior pool of your entity. Save and activate the changes.Implement DeterminationFor additional information on Determinations and its syntax and use you can refer the BDEF ‘/DMO/R_Travel_D’. Alternatively you can also refer https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap100/blob/main/exercises/ex04

Task2: Enhance determination with Joule Code Prediction Capability

Use Joule's Predictive Code Completion feature to complete your code by providing simple prompts for each step in the logic.

Here please note that we should use the Toggle option to disable / enable Joule Code Prediction Capability by pressing  button in the toolbar.

Hint: Disable the Joule Code Prediction Capability if you want to give prompts for new steps / once finished to stop Automatic Code Prediction.

Note:- The logic and examples are mentioned just for your reference and to make the purpose of the challenge easy to understand. Please feel free to use your own logic and dataset.

To know how to use Predictive Code Completion follow this tutorial

Please note that the challenge will remain open for another week for anyone who is running late. 

Before we close for this year we would like to thank everyone who participated. We hope all of you enjoyed the challenge. Let us continue to explore how SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities can support us with our day-to-day tasks when working with ABAP code, to increase our efficiency and to lower total cost of development.

Happy Jouling!!!

entry.title: Re: Understanding GOS Attachments and Their Backend Tables in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Thanks for this valuable article.

Recently, I was working with attachments in MM01/MM02/MM03. In the attachments list, there is an image of the material, and I want to print this attachment on a SmartForm along with the material number.

For example, if one purchase order contains three materials, then all three material images should be printed on the SmartForm. The SmartForm layout is already ready. Could you please help me with how to select the image and  print it on the SmartForm?

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP Databricks Jobs through SAP Datasphere Task Chains
entry.content:
Hi,

In my case, I ran successfully with below body request..

{
"run_name":"run_by_taskchain",
"tasks":[{
"task_key":"run_notebook",
"notebook_task": {
"notebook_path":"/Workspace/Users/<myaccount>/<mynotebook_name>"
},
"timeout_seconds":3600
}],
"max_concurrent_runs":1
}

below parts of body request was not accepted in my databricks environment with below error message in databricks side.

 

"environment_key": "Default""

environments": [{
"environment_key": "Default",
"spec": {
"client": "1"

and you should use "run_name", instead of "name".

entry.title: BDC-SAC] General Security Assessment Checklist for Customer Infrastructure Teams
entry.content:
Q) System access permissions must be assignable at both individual and group levels.
A) Based on the created accounts, permissions can be assigned for SAC standard menus and features, as well as file sharing access. In addition, users who can access the system can be restricted and managed.

Q) Authentication procedures must be implemented for system access.
A) Authentication is performed using an email/password combination, and a SAML 2.0-based Single Sign-On (SSO) authentication process can be configured.

Q) Group password policies, including complexity requirements and periodic changes, must be enforced.
A) Password policies can be configured through options such as password length, validity period, ending character rules, and the use of special characters.

Q) Detailed reasons for authentication failure must not be disclosed.

A) In case of authentication failure, only a generic failure message is displayed, and no information such as inferable password hints is provided.
 
Q) Upon successful login, the system should provide previous login information (e.g., login time and IP address) for the account.
A) Upon user login, login information is available in the SAC Activities menu.
Additionally, audit logs can be accessed via the provided REST API to review IP addresses, as well as successful and failed login attempts.
 
Q) The account must be locked after repeated failed login attempts.
A) After n failed login attempts, the account is locked for n hours.
(Default: 5 attempts, 1 hour)
 
Q) An account unlock function must be implemented upon the user’s request.
A) When changing the account password or unlocking the account, the account can be unlocked through the login account (email) method.
 
Q) A session timeout feature must be enforced.
A) The session timeout duration can be configured in the SAC admin menu.
Additionally, a session timeout can be set for the authentication validity period after login.
 
Q) Authentication information, transaction data in e-commerce, and personal data must be encrypted both at rest and in transit.
A) SAC uses a certificate-based HTTPS secure communication protocol, and all data is encrypted during transmission when communicating with other systems and applications. If an HTTPS secure connection is not established, data cannot be displayed or transmitted/received in SAC.
 
Q) User system access logs must be recorded and retained for at least one year, including login/logout history, account ID, login/logout time, IP address, and success/failure status.
A) This is available in the SAC Activities menu, with a default log retention limit of 500,000 records. Capacity can be increased or extended upon request or through periodic (e.g., annual) arrangements. In addition, the IP addresses of users accessing the system, along with success/failure status, can be verified via the provided REST API.
 
Q) User menu access logs must be recorded and retained for at least one year, including account ID, timestamp, IP address, accessed menu, and activity details (e.g., view, export, download)
A) This is available in the SAC Activities menu, with a default log limit of 500,000 records. Capacity can be expanded upon request or through periodic (e.g., annual) arrangements. In addition, users’ IP addresses, along with success/failure status, can be verified via the provided REST API.

entry.title: Re: OData MCP Proxy - Introduction
entry.content: Great intro to the pattern. One thing I've run into with this approach - once you have 20+ entity sets, maintaining the JSON config gets heavy. Have you thought about auto-reading the EDMX to bootstrap the config? Curious how you handle that at scale.

entry.title: PO text overlapping
entry.content:
Hello Community,

In recent times we are seeing that PO text is overlapping.
Scenario: The PO text is not manually entered it is automatically fetching from api from outside sap.

1. What is maximum length that can accept po text for single row?

2. What are maximum characters accepting that PO text field?

The data coming from api is not in format due to no user discipline.

How to handle this?

entry.title: Re: ME21N Enhancement - Disable/Grey Out Custom Subscreen Field Based on PO Document Type
entry.content: @bogdanbgddm You can add a custom screen in PO using badi me_gui_po_cust. Create a subscreen in your program and register it in the subscribe method by passing program name, screen number, and structure etc. Once the badi is active, the screen will appear in ME21N/ME22N/ME23N.

entry.title: Re: How to Call RESTful APIs from SAP ABAP: A Step-by-Step Guide

entry.title: How do I input an expression using the formula editor in Build Process Automation?
entry.content:
I am trying to create the following process:
① Create a trigger form where text can be entered.
② Add an action group (API - Get) within the automation component.
③ Create an expression editor for the API filter that checks if the input fields (4) from ① match the API fields.


Please provide a formula editor for $filter so that all four fields in the trigger form are equivalent to the fields in API - Get.

Currently, I'm getting an error and can't apply the filter properly.


SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content: Package: ZAC_2273787

entry.title: SCIM3 PATCH API returning 403 Forbidden in SAP Analytics Cloud (GET works, Admin access available)
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I am facing an issue while using the SCIM3 API in SAP Analytics Cloud to update team members using PATCH operation.

API Details

Endpoint:

PATCH  /api/v1/scim3/Groups/<TEAM_UUID>

Authentication:
  • Authorization Bearer Token
Headers Used

  • Authorization: Bearer <access_token>

  • x-csrf-token: <csrf_token>

  • x-sap-sac-custom-auth: true

Payload
"schemas": [
            "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:PatchOp"
        ],
        "Operations": [
            {
                "op":"add",
                "path":"members",
                "value":[
                    {
                        "value":"d0bbac78-776890-9876hjmd",
                        "type":"User",
                        "display":"newuser",
                        "$ref":"/api/v1/scim3/Users/d0bbac78-776890-9876hjmd"
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "op": "replace",
            "path": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group:description",
            "value": "patch test"
            }
        ]
Issue

The PATCH request consistently returns:

{
"status": 403,
"error":"Forbidden"
}

Observations

  • GET operations on /api/v1/scim3/Groups are working successfully.
  • Team UUID is retrieved correctly.
  • Team type is userGroup.
  • I have full admin access (BI_Admin role) in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • OAuth Client is configured with API access with App Integration.
  • CSRF Token is fetched and passed correctly.
  • when CSRF token is removed, the API returns 401 Unauthorized.
  • PATCH always returns 403 Forbidden.
Questions
  1. Are there additional permissions required specifically for SCIM3 PATCH operations ?
  2. Does the OAuth client require explicit SCIM provisioning(write) permissions?
  3. Are there any tenant-level restrictions for SCIM3 write operations ?
Attachments
  • PATCH request (headers + payload)

 

  • Response (403 Forbidden)

Any guidance would be helpful

Thanks!

Even the [entitlement] doesn't have the capacity allocation part, how can I allot org's Quota?

I understand that when creating a subaccount, the organization's quota is automatically assigned. Is that not the case?


I was wondering if I need to buy more separate memory, or if I don't get the screens I need due to permission issues.

entry.title: Re: Error When Reverse GI. Stock Stuck in Blocked Stock I...
entry.content: Hi Laksmipathi, So what should I do with those which already happened?

entry.title: Re: How to create an XML structure of header and items in...
entry.content: Hi, Henrike. Thank you for the information. Appreciate it.

entry.title: Re: Failure o cance GRPO
entry.content:
Cancellation of GRPO requires reversing the original accounting entry. Since no journal entry exists, the system cannot generate a reversal. Additionally, if the GR was posted into a blocked stock / special stock category, cancellation may be restricted until the stock is moved back.

To fix this, verify the following:

1) Check PO Line Item
Verify if the PO item is valuated (check account assignment category, material type, valuation class). If it is non-valuated, no FI posting will occur.

2) Check Material Master
Ensure the material is set as valuated stock. If it is flagged as non-valuated, GR will only update logistics, not accounting.

3) Cancellation Options
If stock was updated, you can reverse the GR in MIGO → Cancellation (movement type 102). If system blocks cancellation, first transfer the stock back to unrestricted (if it went into blocked/special stock). If no stock update occurred, you may need to delete the GR document directly in logistics (no FI impact).

entry.title: Re: Stock Transfer With Delivery
entry.content: Hi Chuma, Thank you. I Appreciate it.

entry.title: Re: API Handling Unit SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content: Hi Dawid, Thank you

entry.title: Re: Validation of document item text does not work with app "Create Supplier Invoice"
entry.content:
Hello @djwafelap

Is your busines requirement to:

  • Have text on all editable invoice items?
  • Or text on all final accounting lines?

Regards

Chris

entry.title: Question Regarding SAPUI5 3D Viewer Background
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the SAPUI5 3D Viewer.

Is it possible to change the background of the scene to a 2D image and then load a 3D CAD file on top of that background?

If possible, could you let me know the method?

Thank you.

entry.title: Down Payment request Not Supported for fixed assets
entry.content: During the creation of a down payment request for an asset PO, we encountered an error indicating that direct posting of down payment to an asset is not supported. Kindly refer to the attached screenshot for further details.

entry.title: Re: Category Management with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Hi Abhijit,

We are referring to the shared link for the configuration and have also checked the error using the “Inspect” option. It shows the following message:
{"error":{"code":"400006","message":"Category not published."}}.

In parallel, we are also working on the service binding.

Thank you for your support.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting with ATP check for MRP Area requirement
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

thanks to @xiuping for this nice blog.

Does anybody know when this feature will be available "The SSCUI assign existing requirement class to  Item Category Types is planned to allow the assignment in 2602."
In our 2602 system, the requirement class SC1 is available, but we cannot assign it to the subcontracting item categories.
Or is it possible via Expert Configuration?

entry.title: Question about SAPUI5 3D Viewer
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the SAPUI5 3D Viewer.

Is it possible to change the scene background by loading a 2D image, and then load a 3D model on top of that background?

If so, could you please explain how to achieve this?

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,
Yonggyu Choi

entry.title: Re: Depersonalization in Test Data Refresh – effects on Procurement Teams
entry.content: Great insight. It was very helpful to understand the impact of depersonalization on procurement workflows and the importance of revalidating team responsibilities after TDR.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
entry.content: Package Name- YZGE291225

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content: Package Created :  ZAC_16246

entry.title: Re: Simplified Maintenance Resource Scheduling - RSH (43R) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: @malaykumarghosh , Excellent explanation about the RSH process.

entry.title: Re: Clarifications on use of app Create Outgoing Invoices
entry.content:
Hi,

Create Outgoing Invoice App is a FI customer invoice creation which uses DR as default document type.

In general the document type is hidden, unless you click on Edition Options and chose 'Document Type is ready for Input'. And if you chose document type SA, system will not allow you to post because document type SA is by standard used for only GL Posting and subledger items like ASeet, Vendor  and Customer are not activated in the document type. You cannot skip subledger posting using SA document type, because the Creating outgoing invoice app is created for subledger purpose but not for general GL Posting.

entry.title: Production process mapping from Finished good (Variant configurable) to SCRAP product ( Non-VC)
entry.content:
In a particular Steel industry, HR Coils are being produced as final product from HOT Metal using production order confirmation (CO11N) against Dummy Sales order.


Details of HR Coils material is as below:
Material: HRC_F
Material Type: FG (Finished Good)
Batch Managed: YES
Variant Configurable: Yes
Pricing: Moving Average Price (V)

Due to handling or ageing issue, user department wants to scrap some of the HR Coils batches, further, to be used in SMS shop (Steel Melting Shop) as BOM material (HRC_SCRAP).
For this new material HRC_SCRAP has been created as below:
Material: HRC_SCRAP
Material Type: SCR
Batch Managed: NO
Variant Configurable: NO
Pricing: Standard Price (S) @RS 35000/MT

Requirement:
1. Production of HRC_SCRAP (1:1) from HRC_F (Batch Managed) has to happen through production confirmation only, against a new dummy scrap sales order.
2. If MIGO is used, no accounting entry should be generated during transfer of HRC_F from one dummy sales order to scrap dummy sales order.
3. Only during confirmation of HRC_SCRAP, accounting entry of HRC_F, HRC_SCRAP and PRD (Price Difference) should be generated.

Kindly help in correct mapping of production process in SAP.

entry.title: Re: Validation of document item text does not work with app "Create Supplier Invoice"
entry.content: Hi Chris, the requirement is to have text on all editable invoice items. Are you referring to the automatically generated tax line item? When I don't enter a tax code, the error message still occurs.
